rationales.hook_strength: '90% of dark spots are the sun' is specific and mildly contrarian against the serum-chasing norm, though the payoff ('protection and patience') is a bit soft for a cover.
rationales.clarity: Every slide is a single actionable idea with a clear label, concrete body copy, and appropriate hedging — no slide tries to do two things.
rationales.audience_fit: Busy 20–35s who've spent money on brightening serums will recognize themselves in slide 1 immediately, and the 'just leave it alone' tone matches their patience level.
rationales.novelty: SPF-first framing is well-worn skincare content; the sleep-skin connection on slide 5 is the freshest angle but isn't leaned into enough to differentiate the deck.
rationales.brand_fit: Claims are hedged throughout ('may help', 'supports', 'slowly'), banned words are absent, the CTA ties sleep/food/habits to the Health Score naturally without AI-cringe.
rationales.predicted_performance: Strong save bait ('save this for later' in caption is well-placed) and a relatable premise should beat average health carousels, but the novelty ceiling limits viral upside.
